name: Ywca Delaware
description_short: YWCA Delaware is a non-profit organization providing free financial counseling, housing assistance, domestic violence support, and career development to women, families, and at-risk individuals across Delaware.
description_long:
YWCA Delaware is a mission-driven non-profit organization dedicated to eliminating racism, empowering women, and promoting peace, justice, freedom and dignity for all. Founded on principles of social change, the organization has established itself as a leading force for economic and social equality in Delaware. The organization serves women, men, and children of all ages, races, religions, and socio-economic backgrounds, with documented impact reaching 9,594 individuals in 2025 alone.

YWCA Delaware offers a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost services spanning financial counseling, housing security, health and safety, career development, and youth empowerment. Their financial counseling program helps clients with homeownership education, foreclosure prevention, money management, and avoiding predatory lending. Housing services include emergency shelter, transitional housing, rapid re-housing, and diversion programs. They operate a 24/7 Sexual Assault Response Center providing free rape crisis and domestic violence counseling statewide. Career services include employment training, job search support, entrepreneurial guidance, and workforce development. Youth programs focus on college and career preparation, leadership development, and self-esteem building.

What distinguishes YWCA Delaware is their integrated approach combining crisis intervention, stabilization services, and long-term economic empowerment under one organization. Their 24/7 crisis helpline (800-773-8570) provides immediate support for sexual assault and domestic violence survivors. The organization explicitly targets low-wage workers and low-income households, with documented outcomes showing 83% of households in their economic advancement programs achieving measurable gains. They also maintain centralized intake for homeless services through a regional housing alliance, ensuring coordinated community response.

For consumers seeking free financial guidance, YWCA Delaware represents a legitimate non-profit alternative to commercial credit counseling, with no evidence of predatory practices or hidden fees. However, as a Delaware-based organization, services are geographically limited to Delaware residents. Their financial counseling, while free, may be less specialized than NFCC-certified agencies in certain areas, and capacity limitations may result in wait times during peak demand periods.
pros: Completely free financial counseling services with no hidden fees or commercial motives
24/7 crisis helpline (800-773-8570) providing immediate support for sexual assault and domestic violence survivors
Integrated housing services including emergency shelter, transitional housing, and rapid re-housing programs
Statewide coverage across Delaware with comprehensive geographic reach
Documented measurable outcomes with 83% of economic advancement program participants achieving financial stability gains
Multi-service model combining crisis intervention, stabilization, and long-term economic empowerment
Centralized intake coordination for homeless services through Housing Alliance Delaware partnership
cons: Services limited to Delaware residents only; no out-of-state assistance available
Capacity limitations may result in wait times for financial counseling during peak demand periods
Website does not specify NFCC certification status or credential details for financial counselors
Limited information about typical counseling engagement timelines or service duration expectations
No clear pricing structure or fee disclosure for any services (though stated as free)
best_for: Delaware women and families facing housing instability or homelessness needing emergency shelter and transitional housing
Low-income individuals seeking free financial counseling for homeownership, foreclosure prevention, or money management
Sexual assault and domestic violence survivors needing 24/7 crisis support and confidential counseling
Low-wage workers in Delaware pursuing career development, job training, and economic advancement opportunities
diagnosis: YWCA Delaware is best for Delaware residents seeking free, mission-driven financial counseling integrated with housing stability and safety services. Primary caveat: geographic limitation to Delaware only, and potential wait times for counseling due to non-profit capacity constraints.
services: Free financial counseling for homeownership education and preparation
Foreclosure prevention and loss mitigation counseling
Money management and financial literacy training
24/7 Sexual Assault Response Center with rape crisis counseling
Domestic violence crisis helpline and support services
Emergency shelter for homeless women and families
Transitional housing programs (SCHE Joint Transitional Housing)
Rapid re-housing assistance for formerly homeless individuals
Housing diversion programs to prevent homelessness
Employment training and job search support
Career navigation and job placement assistance
Youth college and career preparation programs
Youth leadership development and diversity workshops
Basic needs assistance including food and hygiene support
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
